Strip_Summary: POV from standing position in mobile operations room. Third commander in nine months.
Stickman at briefing table  -  two predecessors didn't retire. Dim bunker atmosphere.
Empty chair with folded uniforms  -  supreme authority dead. Same missile wave.
Two folded uniforms on armrests with medals pinned, coffee cups untouched. Nobody moved them.
Sixty-seven years old. Called back because the list ran out.
Stickman standing beside chair at head of table, two fingers touching backrest like checking pulse.
Red Notice document  -  190 countries. Interpol warrant on corkboard.
Bold white text on dark military-green background: 'But here  -  you are the law.'
Two panels: boy named after grandfather in southern city (left), young man erasing name at 21 (right).
Mother calling old name for years. One correction. She stops calling entirely. Warm amber glow.
Young stickman joining revolutionary guard on first day. Barracks gate, recruits lining up.
Guard not regular army  -  answers to revolution, takes orders from God. Contrast with government military.
Eight years in trenches. Foot soldier to battalion commander. Sandbags and barbed wire.
Asymmetric warfare doctrine  -  strike from angle they forgot to model. War lesson.
Three photographs from war  -  not victories, three men couldn't save. Photos in left chest pocket over heart.
Chemical weapons attack  -  men choking with foam on lips. Western neighbor's weapons.
Dying soldier gripping stickman wrist. Grip stronger than expected. Remembered for life. Warm amber edges.
Doctoral thesis on asymmetric warfare. Professors give honors  -  don't know written from memory.
Stickman reading case files while other officers drink tea in military lounge.
Twelve-page proposal on borrowed typewriter. Force for wars country cannot officially fight.
War ends. Everyone goes home. Stickman submits proposal for unofficial warfare unit.
1988  -  founding commander. Approval stamp on founding documents. Map on wall.
Project force without fingerprints. Training fighters loyal to cause not flag.
Operatives with suitcases  -  cash, diplomatic pouches, loyalty in bones. Airport terminal.
Unit with no name, no address, no website. World feels effects but never traces source.
Map showing merged cell network  -  scattered cells into one organism across mountain country.
Training camps disguised as farms. Militias disguised as charities. Empire on faith and phone number.
Embassy explosion in South America  -  1992. Twenty-nine dead. Smoke rising.
Community center same city  -  1994. Eighty-five dead. Crater.
Families, children. Building turned to crater in neighborhood unrelated to country.
Morning after  -  rescue workers pulling bodies three days. Youngest victim at language lesson.
Attribution to unit. Five words. Heavy silence.
Reading briefing standing up. Folding. Placing on left side of desk  -  never read again, never discard. Grey vignette.
Never confirm, never deny. Return to headquarters. Continue building as if crater is weather far away.
1997 handover  -  unit to successor. Will become most famous military figure. Walks airports openly.
Bold text on dark background: 'You warned him about that.'
Red Notice irony  -  warrant trapping inside country is proof of competence. Best performance review.
2007. International police publishes photograph. Red Notice. 190 countries.
Missiles procured now will be fired without permission decade later. Don't know yet.
Cannot leave country. Don't need to. Everything operates without passport.
Machine doesn't require physical presence. Design becomes prison. Tension blue wash.
2009 defense minister  -  signing billions in contracts. Engineers showing warhead trajectories.
2021 interior minister. 2022 women pulling scarves, dancing, shouting dead girl's name.
Directing response. Rubber bullets then real ones. Country burns. Signing operational guidelines.
Girl's name becomes chant. Stickman's name on no banner. Chant echoing.
Sitting alone in dark room lights off thirty minutes. Processing. Cataloging vacancy implications. Dread grey.
Lights come on. Tactician again. Transition from darkness.
Night casualty report review. Names unrecognized. Ages that shouldn't be listed. Close folder, open next.
Successor at foreign unit killed by drone strike 2020. Legend destroyed.
Watching drone footage on phone in bathroom. Standing alone. Replay once. Pocket phone. Wash hands. Bittersweet amber.
June 2025  -  first guard commander killed. Chair empty before fires out.
December 2025 deputy appointment. Seven officers in container room. No photo. One handshake. Different exit door.
Bold text: 'If the next strike comes  -  you are what remains.'
February 28  -  largest coordinated strike. Three waves calibrated to hit rescue teams.
Basement when first concussion wave hits. Water in glass rippling. Not close enough to shake table.
Second commander dead. Chief of staff dead. Defense minister dead. Supreme authority dead. Same hour.
Hands stop. Knuckles press white against table edge. Then breathe. Hands move again. Dread grey.
Survived  -  not at compound, not at ministry. Undocumented basement saved life.
Forty years shadow networks. Changed name at 21. Never same chair twice. Shadow was always him. Blue wash.
March 1 phone call in diesel bunker. Commander-in-chief. Phone rang three times before answer.
Force with no chain of command. Every link severed. Offices are craters. Chart like bombed building.
Whiteboard  -  new decentralized structure. No hierarchy, no central node. Kill one, rest continue.
President speaks proportional response. International law for countries at tables. This country under table since 21.
Never built for thrones. Built for rooms without windows where men point at maps. Tension blue.
1988 architecture  -  cells surviving decapitation. Never imagined applying to own country.
Civilian president calls about restraint. Almost says something. Doesn't know what. Hangs up.
Dead supreme authority's son appointed successor. Stickman making calls. Council votes as instructed.
Son somewhere in bunker, wounded. Posters printed with title not face.
Three AM silent screens. Thinking about South America building. Not explosion  -  the morning after.
Dust in air. Rescue workers moving slowly. Someone calling name that never answers.
Eighty-five people, never learned names. Eyes closed trying to remember one name.
Bold text: 'You get close. You stop.'
Missiles arcing over Gulf like geometry. Clean lines. Math is beautiful. Ground impact is not.
Week six  -  2000+ civilians dead. 3.2 million displaced.
Infrastructure collapsing  -  water offline, power at 30%, hospitals on fumes, ambulances turned away.
Retaliatory strikes on Gulf bases. Missiles from decade-old procurement. Handwriting on original form.
Hospital footage on screen. Ceiling tiles on operating table. Nurse with phone flashlight. Surgeon tying artery.
Strait closed, oil spikes. Nurse's hand shaking same as dying trench soldier. Same grip opposite direction. Amber.
Launch alert on screen. Unapproved coordinates.
Six screens three radios officer shouting  -  staring at numbers not understanding. Then understanding.
Looking at officer  -  twenty-six, trained his father. Father died second wave six weeks ago.
Bold text: 'He doesn't look back. He doesn't need to.'
System worked. No single failure point. No permission required. Staring at screen too long.
First time realization  -  no longer in command.
Bold text: 'For a second  -  you almost give an order. You don't.'
Hospital direct hit  -  surgeons operating when lights went out. Finished by phone light.
People dying in dark  -  not front line, in apartments, kitchens. Waiting for water that doesn't come.
System answers every strike. Missile generates targeting package generates launch order. One response then another.
Stops being response, becomes pattern. Missiles, displacement, recruitment, back again.
Pattern seen on paper looks cleaner. On screen it doesn't stop.
Loop designed 1988 on typewriter. Forty years later runs on satellites and bodies of strangers. Dread grey.
Bold text: 'You just never thought you'd be inside it.'
Right thumb rubbing index finger  -  trench habit. Officer staring at hand. Stops.
Nobody sits when stickman stands. Not rank  -  what happened to men who sat.
Operations room  -  different basement, same war. Callback to opening scene.
Eighty-five people. Girl in station. Man in trench who grabbed wrist. Memory fragments.
Bold text: 'You remember the grip. You never learned the names.'
Two fingers on chair backrest. Thumb rubbing index finger. Nobody watching.
Warrant still active. Courtroom still empty.
Engine still running. Knows exactly how it works. Designed every part. Every consequence.
Bold text: 'You just never designed a way out. Not for them. Not for you.'
